title: Lesson 40 — Beginnings and Endings

words.definition: the establishment or beginning of something
words.example: The company has grown steadily since its inception.

words.definition: the origin or formation of something
words.example: The idea had its genesis in a late-night conversation.

words.definition: the highest or final point, reached after a long process
words.example: The award was the culmination of decades of research.

words.definition: the final resolution of a story's plot; the outcome of a situation
words.example: The novel's denouement resolved every loose thread.

words.definition: a person or thing that comes before another of the same kind; a forerunner
words.example: The telegraph was a precursor to modern telecommunications.

words.definition: a person or thing that signals the approach of something
words.example: The robin is often called a harbinger of spring.

words.definition: a sign or warning that something significant is about to happen
words.example: Sailors read the strange sky as a portent of storms.

words.definition: an event regarded as a sign of future good or evil
words.example: A black cat crossing the path was taken as a bad omen.

words.definition: to be a warning or indication of a future event
words.example: The opening scene foreshadows the tragedy to come.

words.definition: an event or action that precedes and prepares for a more important one
words.example: The small protest was a prelude to a much larger movement.

words.definition: a short final section at the end of a book or play
words.example: The epilogue revealed what became of each character.

words.definition: the consequences or after-effects of a significant event
words.example: In the aftermath of the flood, volunteers rebuilt the school.

story: Historians still argue about the empire's exact [[inception|inception]], though most trace its [[genesis|genesis]] to a single treaty. Its [[culmination|culmination]] came two centuries later, and its slow collapse forms the [[denouement|denouement]] of every textbook chapter on the era. Scholars call the earlier city-states a [[precursor|precursor]] to the empire, and one border skirmish is often read as a [[harbinger|harbinger]] of what followed — a [[portent|portent]], almost an [[omen|omen]], that seemed to [[foreshadow|foreshadow]] the wars to come. That skirmish was merely a [[prelude|prelude]] to centuries of conflict, and even the empire's [[epilogue|epilogue]] — its final quiet decades — could not undo the [[aftermath|aftermath]] of its fall.
